skill.name: cloud-infrastructure
skill.description: Cloud infrastructure design guidance for AWS, Azure, and GCP, including architecture patterns, Infrastructure as Code practices, cost optimization, availability, security, and monitoring.
skill.summary: Plan secure, reliable, and cost-aware cloud infrastructure across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and appear to be false positives from Markdown formatting, cloud provider terminology, and normal infrastructure guidance. No executable scripts, command invocation logic, credential collection, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged backticks are Markdown fences or inline formatting for a project tree and reference filenames. They do not execute commands and no shell interpreter is invoked.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are Markdown documentation, not Ruby or shell code. There is no surrounding executable language context or command execution pathway.

low_findings.title: Cloud Service Name Misclassified as C2 Keyword
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged term appears in a cloud compute comparison table as EC2. This is an AWS service name, not command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line lists AWS, Azure, and GCP virtual machine offerings in a documentation table. No network callback, malware coordination, or hidden endpoint is present.

low_findings.title: Infrastructure Wording Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines contain ordinary words such as description, designing, and design, or unrelated cloud infrastructure text. No weak cryptographic algorithm is recommended or implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is prose and headings, not cryptographic configuration or code. The only encryption guidance in the file recommends KMS and TLS.

low_findings.title: Security Guidance Misclassified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines are defensive architecture guidance about identity, access, VPN, and direct connectivity. They do not instruct system probing or network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of a security best practices checklist. No reconnaissance commands, scanners, targets, or enumeration workflow appear in the file.

content.value_statement: Cloud teams need clear patterns for reliable, cost-aware infrastructure. This skill guides architecture, IaC, security, availability, monitoring, and cost decisions across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
actual_capabilities: Compares compute, storage, and database patterns across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
Outlines Terraform project structure, module design, and remote state practices.
Provides cost optimization tactics for compute, storage, and network usage.
Describes high availability patterns for multi-AZ and multi-region deployments.
Lists defensive security practices for identity, network controls, and data protection.
Identifies monitoring metrics and alerting practices for cloud operations.
limitations: Does not include executable Terraform modules or deployment scripts.
Does not validate live cloud accounts, permissions, quotas, or current pricing.
Does not replace provider documentation for service limits or compliance rules.
Mentions reference files that are not included in the scanned file structure.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Cloud Architecture
use_cases.description: Map application needs to compute, storage, database, networking, availability, and monitoring patterns.

use_cases.title: Standardize Infrastructure as Code
use_cases.description: Define Terraform structure, module boundaries, state storage, and environment separation before implementation.

use_cases.title: Reduce Cloud Operating Costs
use_cases.description: Review compute, storage, and network choices for practical savings opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Cloud Services
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new workload
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose cloud services for this application: [describe workload]. Compare AWS, Azure, and GCP options for compute, storage, database, and networking.

prompt_templates.title: Design Terraform Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Terraform project structure for [environment count] environments and [main services]. Include modules, state management, variables, and outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Review High Availability
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this cloud architecture for high availability: [describe architecture]. Identify single points of failure, failover needs, and multi-region options.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Multi-Cloud Operations
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced cost, resilience, and security planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this multi-cloud infrastructure plan: [paste plan]. Recommend improvements for cost, security, monitoring, data protection, and operational resilience.

output_examples.input: Design a production web application on AWS with high availability and managed data services.
output_examples.output: A concise architecture outline with load balancing, multi-AZ compute, managed relational storage, private networking, backups, monitoring, and cost controls.

output_examples.input: Review our Terraform approach for dev, staging, and production.
output: Separate state per environment with remote locking.
Shared modules for networking, compute, and databases.
Clear input variables, documented outputs, and versioned module releases.

output_examples.input: Find cloud cost reduction options for a growing analytics workload.
output_examples.output: A prioritized cost review covering instance sizing, reserved capacity, lifecycle policies, data transfer paths, and unused resource cleanup.

best_practices: Provide workload requirements, traffic patterns, data sensitivity, and recovery targets before asking for architecture guidance.
Ask for provider-specific tradeoffs when comparing AWS, Azure, and GCP services.
Validate generated recommendations against current provider documentation and your internal compliance standards.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the skill as a live cloud scanner or deployment automation tool.
Do not paste secrets, account credentials, or private keys into prompts.
Do not copy architecture guidance directly into production without review and testing.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy cloud resources?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning and design guidance, but it does not run deployment commands.

faq.question: Which cloud providers does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers AWS, Azure, and GCP patterns for common infrastructure decisions.

faq.question: Can it help with Terraform?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes project structure, module design, remote state, and environment separation.

faq.question: Can it estimate exact monthly cost?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest cost optimization areas, but exact estimates require current pricing and usage data.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated environments?
faq.answer: It includes general security and compliance topics, but regulated workloads need formal review.

faq.question: Does it include hidden commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The scanned skill content is Markdown guidance without executable scripts.
